483	\section{Affine acyclicity of the {\v C}ech complex}
484	
485	\begin{definition}
486	[Standard affine cover from a spanning family]
487	  \label{def:standard_affine_cover}
488	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop}
489	  \mathlibok
490	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open}).}
491	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring (so \(R = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R,
492	  \mathcal{O})\)) and let \(s : \iota \to R\) be a family of elements that generate
493	  the unit ideal, \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) = \top\). The
494	  \emph{standard affine open cover} of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) associated with
495	  \(s\) is the affine open cover whose index set is \(\iota\), whose \(i\)-th member
496	  is the basic open \(D(s_i)\), and whose \(i\)-th structure morphism is the map of
497	  affine schemes induced by the localisation homomorphism \(R \to R_{s_i}\); that
498	  is, in Mathlib notation the defining equation
499	  \(\bigl(\text{affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop}\bigr)_f\, i =
500	  \operatorname{Spec.map}\bigl(\text{algebraMap}\; R\; (\operatorname{Localization.Away}(s_i))\bigr)\),
501	  so that the sections over the \(i\)-th piece are the \emph{away} localisation
502	  \(R_{s_i} = \operatorname{Localization.Away}(s_i)\) and the geometric piece is
503	  \(D(s_i)\). This is exactly the standard open covering
504	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R = \bigcup_i D(s_i)\) by basic opens, packaged as a single
505	  cover object. It is the spanning-family bundle \((s, hs)\) that the affine
506	  {\v C}ech vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is stated over.
507	\end{definition}
508	
509	\subsection{The categorical--module bridge (L1)}
510	\label{sec:cech_l1_bridge}
511	
512	The affine vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is a statement about the
513	\emph{section} {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
514	\mathcal{F})\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} --- a cochain complex of
515	abelian groups --- whereas the combinatorial contracting-homotopy core operates on a
516	concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules. The passage between the two, the
517	\emph{categorical--module bridge}, decomposes into two self-contained statements: the
518	section identification (each section group is an away localisation, with the
519	restriction maps the canonical localisation maps) and the homology-to-exactness
520	reduction (vanishing of the abstract homology is equivalent to exactness of the
521	underlying localised-module complex). These are isolated as
522	Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} and
523	Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} below; the assembly --- feeding each
524	positive-degree node through the local-to-global exactness criterion and closing with
525	the dependent combinatorial port --- is carried out in the proof of
526	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}.
527	
528	\begin{definition}
529	
530	\leanok
531	[Quasi-coherent sections over a basic open are an away localisation]
532	  \label{def:qcoh_sections_localized}
533	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.qcohSectionsAwayLocalized}
534	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:standard_affine_cover}
535	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Lemma \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
536	  % items (4)--(5) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L701--707).
537	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "(4) For every $f\in R$ we have $\Gamma(D(f), \widetilde M) = M_f$
538	  % as an $R_f$-module.
539	  % (5) Whenever $D(g) \subset D(f)$ the restriction mappings
540	  % on $\mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}$ and $\widetilde M$
541	  % are the maps
542	  % $R_f \to R_g$ and $M_f \to M_g$ from Lemma
543	  % \ref{lemma-standard-open}."
544	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
545	  items (4)--(5).}
546	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
547	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R}\)-module, and write
548	  \(M = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})\) for its module of global
549	  sections. For every \(g \in R\), the restriction-of-sections map
550	  \(M = \mathcal{F}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \to \mathcal{F}(D(g))\) exhibits the
551	  section group \(\mathcal{F}(D(g))\) as the away localisation \(M_g\) of \(M\); that
552	  is, this map is an \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) for the multiplicative
553	  submonoid \(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} g\) of powers of \(g\). Moreover, for
554	  \(D(g_2) \subseteq D(g_1)\) the restriction map
555	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(g_1)) \to \mathcal{F}(D(g_2))\) is, under these identifications, the
556	  canonical localisation map \(M_{g_1} \to M_{g_2}\) (the unique \(R\)-linear map
557	  compatible with the localisation units). Item~(4) of Tag~01HV gives the first
558	  assertion for \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) (\(\Gamma(D(g), \widetilde{M}) = M_g\)
559	  as an \(R_g\)-module) and item~(5) gives the second (the restriction along
560	  \(D(g) \subset D(f)\) is the localisation map \(M_f \to M_g\)).
561	
562	  The module-level input on the side of Mathlib is the development
563	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde}: the comparison map
564	  \((\operatorname{tilde.toOpen} M\, (D(g))).\mathrm{hom}\) carries the instance
565	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} g)\), and
566	  \(\operatorname{isUnit\_algebraMap\_end\_basicOpen}\) records that \(g\) acts
567	  invertibly on the sections over \(D(g)\); the restriction compatibility is
568	  \(\operatorname{tilde.toOpen\_res}\). These supply the statement \emph{verbatim} for
569	  the standard sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\). For an \emph{arbitrary} quasi-coherent
570	  \(\mathcal{F}\) the remaining step is the canonical comparison
571	  \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})}\), i.e.\
572	  that the counit \(\operatorname{Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism. This
573	  is \emph{not} yet available in Mathlib for a general quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\):
574	  Mathlib provides it only from a \emph{global} presentation
575	  (\(\operatorname{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_of\_presentation}\)), whereas quasi-coherence
576	  supplies only \emph{local} presentation data on a cover. Globalising the local data
577	  on the affine \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) --- the affine equivalence
578	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \simeq \operatorname{Mod}(R)\), Stacks
579	  Tag~01I8 --- is the one genuine gap this definition records as a build target; it is
580	  the canonical missing affine equivalence and is self-contained.
581	\end{definition}
582	
583	\begin{lemma}
584	[Section {\v C}ech homology vanishes iff the localised complex is exact]
585	  \label{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}
586	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_homology_exact,
587	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts,
588	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.isUnit_powers,
589	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.mul,
590	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd,
591	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison,
592	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_apply,
593	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp,
594	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply,
595	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp_structure,
596	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_self,

623	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
624	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (identification with the
625	  complex of localisations).}
626	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec}
627	  R}\)-module and \(\mathcal{U}\) the standard cover associated with a spanning family
628	  \(s : \iota \to R\). Under the section identification of
629	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}, the section {\v C}ech complex
630	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
631	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} is isomorphic, as a cochain complex, to
632	  the concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules
633	  \[
634	    D^\bullet : \quad
635	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
636	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0,1\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
637	    \cdots,
638	    \qquad
639	    s_\sigma = \prod_{k} s_{\sigma(k)},
640	  \]
641	  whose differential is the alternating sum of the canonical localisation maps. For
642	  each \(p\), vanishing of the abstract homology is equivalent to exactness of the
643	  underlying \(R\)-module maps,
644	  \[
645	    \operatorname{IsZero}\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
646	      \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p\bigr)
647	    \;\Longleftrightarrow\;
648	    \operatorname{Function.Exact}\bigl(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to D^{p+1}\bigr).
649	  \]
650	  The two directions are supplied by Mathlib: \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the
651	  homology is \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) at \(p\)
652	  (\(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\)), and \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\)
653	  of a short complex of \(R\)-modules is \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) of the
654	  underlying linear maps (\(\operatorname{ShortComplex.moduleCat\_exact\_iff}\)).
655	\end{lemma}
656	
657	\begin{proof}
658	  \uses{def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_complex}
659	  The term identification is the degreewise statement of
660	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: the degree-\(p\) section group
661	  \(\prod_\sigma \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is \(\prod_\sigma M_{s_\sigma}\), since
662	  each \((p+1)\)-fold intersection \(D(s_{\sigma(0)}) \cap \cdots \cap
663	  D(s_{\sigma(p)})\) is the basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product
664	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\) and the sections there are the away
665	  localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\). The differential identification is item~(5) of
666	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: each face restriction
667	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j})) \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is the
668	  canonical localisation map \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\), so the
669	  alternating sum of restrictions is the alternating sum of localisation maps, the
670	  differential of \(D^\bullet\). This gives the isomorphism of cochain complexes
671	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \cong D^\bullet\). The
672	  homology equivalence then follows by transporting along this isomorphism and
673	  applying \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\) (\(\operatorname{IsZero}\)
674	  homology \(\Leftrightarrow\) \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\)) and
675	  \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.moduleCat\_exact\_iff}\) (\(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) of
676	  \(R\)-modules \(\Leftrightarrow\) \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\)).
677	
678	  The identification of the abstract section complex with the concrete localised
679	  complex \(D^\bullet\) --- together with the construction of the prepend transport
680	  \(c\) and the localisation cofaces \(\delta_k\) discharging the unit, shift, and
681	  coface-commutation compatibilities \(\mathtt{hu}\)/\(\mathtt{hsh}\)/\(\mathtt{hcomm}\)
682	  of the dependent combinatorial port --- is the bulk of the bridge. The non-circular
683	  route is reflection along the localisation equivalence on its essential image (the
684	  global-sections functor \(\Gamma\) is the inverse equivalence there, so it reflects
685	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of homology); it is \emph{not} the assertion that
686	  ``\(\Gamma\) preserves homology'', which would presuppose the very affine exactness
687	  being proved.
688	\end{proof}

771	
772	\leanok
773	[Positive-degree exactness of the section {\v C}ech module complex]
774	  \label{lem:section_cech_module_exact}
775	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact}
776	  \uses{def:section_cech_module_complex}
777	  With the notation of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex}, the
778	  un-localised section {\v C}ech module complex \(D^\bullet\) is exact in positive
779	  degrees: for every \(p\),
780	  \[
781	    \operatorname{Function.Exact}\bigl(d^{p+1} : D^{p+1} \to D^{p+2},\;
782	      d^{p+2} : D^{p+2} \to D^{p+3}\bigr).
783	  \]
784	  This is step~(a) of the affine vanishing: the node-by-node exactness of the
785	  un-localised module complex, from which the homology vanishing of the section
786	  {\v C}ech complex (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}, step~(c)) is read off.
787	\end{lemma}
788	
789	\begin{proof}
790	  \uses{def:section_cech_module_complex}
791	  \leanok
792	  Exactness of \(D^\bullet\) at each positive-degree node is a local-to-global
793	  statement over the spanning family. By the local-to-global exactness criterion
794	  \(\operatorname{exact\_of\_isLocalized\_span}(\operatorname{Set.range} s)\) (with the
795	  spanning hypothesis \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) = \top\)), it
796	  suffices to verify \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(d^{p+1}, d^{p+2})\) after
797	  localising at each spanning element \(s_r\). In the localisation at \(s_r\) the
798	  comparison family \(\mathtt{map\_dDiff\_eq\_locDiff}\) identifies the localised
799	  differential of \(D^\bullet\) with the localised Čech differential \(\mathtt{locDiff}\)
800	  of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex} (using the localisation
801	  transitivity \(M_a[1/b] = M_{ab}\) of
802	  \(\mathtt{AwayComparison.comparison\_isLocalizedModule}\)). Exactness of that
803	  localised differential is \(\mathtt{locDiff\_exact}\), which is in turn the
804	  dependent-coefficient combinatorial exactness transported along
805	  \(\mathtt{locDiff\_eq\_depDiff}\); the contracting homotopy at the fixed index
806	  \(r\) (where \(s_r\) is invertible) furnishes \((dh + hd) = \operatorname{id}\),
807	  giving the localised exactness one localisation at a time. Reassembling over the
808	  spanning family yields the un-localised exactness.
809	\end{proof}

960	  dependent-coefficient combinatorial cores and the spanning-family certifier are
961	  already in place; the section and differential identifications of
962	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} and the homology--exactness reduction of
963	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} are the bridge that connects them to the
964	  abstract section complex.
965	
966	  The homotopy is built directly from the away-localisation identification, \emph{not}
967	  routed through \(\operatorname{SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy}\): the
968	  extra-degeneracy packaging of the contracting homotopy is a documented dead end for
969	  this cover (the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) map is only a section-level homotopy
970	  after localisation, not an augmented-simplicial extra degeneracy), so the concrete
971	  \(\operatorname{depHomotopy}\)/\(\operatorname{depDiff}\) route of the dependent port
972	  is used instead.
973	\end{proof}
974	
975	\subsection{Presheaf-level {\v C}ech machinery}
976	\label{sec:presheaf_cech_machinery}
977	
978	The {\v C}ech-acyclicity of injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
979	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} below) is not a statement about a single
980	affine cover: it is proved on the abelian category \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)
981	of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, where the {\v C}ech complex of sections
982	is the \(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex of an explicit free resolution. The argument
983	rests on two presheaf-level facts, recorded here, and one transfer of injectivity.
984	First, a free complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of presheaves of modules whose
985	\(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex into \(\mathcal{F}\) \emph{is} the {\v C}ech complex
986	of sections (Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex},
987	Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} and
988	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_hom_identification}). Second, the resolution property of
989	that complex: \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) resolves \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\)
990	concentrated in degree \(0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso}). Together
991	these give: an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is injective as a presheaf
992	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} via
993	Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint} applied to the sheafification adjunction
994	Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}), so \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, \mathcal{I})\) is
995	exact and carries the augmented resolution to an exact section complex, i.e.\
996	\(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\). This direct route uses
997	neither enough-injectives in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) nor a right-derived
998	\(\delta\)-functor identification.
999	
1000	\begin{definition}
1001	
1002	[Free presheaf complex of a cover]
1003	  \label{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
1004	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreePresheafComplex,
1005	    AlgebraicGeometry.freeYoneda,
1006	    AlgebraicGeometry.coverOpen,
1007	    AlgebraicGeometry.coverInterOpen,
1008	    AlgebraicGeometry.coverInterOpen_comp_le,
1009	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeSimplicial,
1010	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreePresheafComplex_X,
1011	    AlgebraicGeometry.sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport}
1012	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-cech-map-into}
1013	  % (the complex $K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet$) (read from
1014	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1142--1162, with the $j_{p!}$ description
1015	  % L1095--1135).
1016	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Consider the chain complex $K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet$
1017	  % of presheaves of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules
1018	  % $$
1019	  % \ldots
1020	  % \to
1021	  % \bigoplus_{i_0i_1i_2} (j_{i_0i_1i_2})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0i_1i_2}}
1022	  % \to
1023	  % \bigoplus_{i_0i_1} (j_{i_0i_1})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0i_1}}
1024	  % \to
1025	  % \bigoplus_{i_0} (j_{i_0})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0}}
1026	  % \to 0 \to \ldots
1027	  % $$
1028	  % where the last nonzero term is placed in degree $0$
1029	  % and where the map
1030	  % $$
1031	  % (j_{i_0\ldots i_{p + 1}})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0\ldots i_{p + 1}}}
1032	  % \longrightarrow
1033	  % (j_{i_0\ldots \hat i_j \ldots i_{p + 1}})_{p!}
1034	  % \mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0\ldots \hat i_j \ldots i_{p + 1}}}
1035	  % $$
1036	  % is given by $(-1)^j$ times the canonical map."
1037	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology,
1038	  \texttt{lemma-cech-map-into} (the free complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\)).}
1039	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an
1040	  open covering, with \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\)
1041	  the open immersion of each finite intersection. The Stacks formulation uses, for
1042	  each intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\), the \emph{extension-by-zero} presheaf
1043	  \((j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_{!}\,\mathcal{O}_X|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\), the presheaf of
1044	  modules sending an open \(W\) to \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\) when
1045	  \(W \subset U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) and to \(0\) otherwise. This extension-by-zero
1046	  presheaf is, canonically, the \emph{free presheaf of modules on the representable}
1047	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\): writing
1048	  \(\mathbf{y}\,V = \operatorname{Hom}(-, V)\) for the Yoneda presheaf of the open
1049	  \(V\) on the site of opens of \(X\), the free presheaf of modules
1050	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V)\) sends \(W\) to the free
1051	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\)-module on the set \(\operatorname{Hom}(W, V)\), which is
1052	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\) if \(W \subset V\) and \(0\) otherwise — exactly
1053	  extension-by-zero of \(\mathcal{O}_X|_V\). Its defining universal property
1054	  \[
1055	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)}\bigl(
1056	      \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V),\, \mathcal{F}\bigr)
1057	    \;\cong\; \mathcal{F}(V)
1058	  \]
1059	  is the free–forgetful adjunction for presheaves of modules composed with the
1060	  Yoneda isomorphism \(\operatorname{Hom}(\mathbf{y}\,V, G) \cong G(V)\). The
1061	  \emph{free presheaf complex} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the chain complex
1062	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) in the abelian category
1063	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules whose
1064	  term in degree \(p\) is the direct sum
1065	  \[
1066	    K(\mathcal{U})_p
1067	      = \bigoplus_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
1068	        \operatorname{free}\bigl(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
1069	  \]
1070	  with \(K(\mathcal{U})_p\) placed in degree \(p \geq 0\) (and \(0\) in negative
1071	  degrees), and whose differential \(K(\mathcal{U})_{p+1} \to K(\mathcal{U})_p\) is
1072	  the alternating sum \(\sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\) of the maps
1073	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}) \to
1074	  \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}})\)
1075	  obtained by applying \(\operatorname{free}\) to the representable index-dropping
1076	  maps \(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} \to \mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots
1077	  \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}\) induced by the inclusions of opens
1078	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} \subset U_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}\)
1079	  that drop the index \(i_j\). Each summand is a \emph{projective} object of
1080	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\), because the universal property above identifies
1081	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots
1082	  i_p}), -)\) with the exact ``sections over \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\)'' functor
1083	  \((-)(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\); this is the structural feature exploited below.
1084	
1085	  We assume the index set \(I\) is \emph{finite} (so the required coproducts exist in
1086	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\); this matches the finiteness hypothesis of the
1087	  comparison target). Here the direct sum \(\bigoplus\) is the categorical coproduct
1088	  \(\coprod\) (the indexed \(\operatorname{Sigma}\) colimit), which for a finite index
1089	  set coincides with the finite biproduct.
1090	\end{definition}

1124	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of presheaves.}
1125	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an open
1126	  covering. For a presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{F}\), the
1127	  \emph{section {\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
1128	  \mathcal{F})\) is the cochain complex of \emph{abelian groups} whose term in
1129	  degree \(p\) is the product of sections over the \((p+1)\)-fold intersections,
1130	  \[
1131	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
1132	      = \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
1133	        \mathcal{F}\bigl(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),

1136	  (the evaluation of \(\mathcal{F}\) at the open \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\)), with
1137	  differential the alternating sum of restriction maps

1140	      = \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\,
1141	        s_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}
1142	        \big|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}}.
1143	  \]

1145	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of

1147	  \(\prod (f|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\) in
1148	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\), an object of relative sheaves over the base, whereas the
1149	  section {\v C}ech complex here is the simpler complex of \emph{global sections} of
1150	  \(\mathcal{F}\) over the intersections, an object of abelian groups (the underlying
1151	  additive structure of the sections). The two must not be conflated: the
1152	  presheaf-level acyclicity argument below runs entirely on this section complex.

1221	\begin{proof}
1222	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:section_cech_complex}
1223	  The free–forgetful adjunction for presheaves of modules gives, composed with the
1224	  Yoneda isomorphism \(\operatorname{Hom}(\mathbf{y}\,V, \mathcal{F}) \cong
1225	  \mathcal{F}(V)\), the natural identification, for each multi-index,
1226	  \[
1227	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}\bigl(
1228	      \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}),\, \mathcal{F}
1229	    \bigr)
1230	    \;\cong\;
1231	    \operatorname{Hom}\bigl(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p},\,
1232	      \mathcal{F}\bigr)
1233	    \;\cong\;
1234	    \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}),
1235	  \]
1236	  where the last group is the evaluation of \(\mathcal{F}\) at the open
1237	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\). Taking the product over multi-indices,
1238	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(K(\mathcal{U})_p, \mathcal{F}) =
1239	  \prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}) =

1241	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_p\) therefore represents the functor \(\mathcal{F} \mapsto
1242	  \prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\). Under this
1243	  identification the \((-1)^j\) maps \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0
1244	  \ldots i_{p+1}}) \to \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j}
1245	  \ldots i_{p+1}})\) defining the differential of \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) become
1246	  exactly the alternating-sum restriction differential of
1247	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\), so the two cochain
1248	  complexes coincide; naturality in \(\mathcal{F}\) is immediate from the
1249	  naturality of the free–forgetful adjunction and of Yoneda. The per-multi-index
1250	  bijection \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,
1251	  U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}), \mathcal{F}) \cong \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\) is additive,
1252	  so it is promoted to an isomorphism of abelian groups (an \emph{additive}
1253	  equivalence); taking the product over multi-indices and assembling these degreewise
1254	  additive isomorphisms — which commute with the alternating-sum differentials — yields
1255	  the complex isomorphism by matching components degree by degree.
1256	\end{proof}

1299	\begin{lemma}
1300	[Objectwise detection of quasi-isomorphisms of presheaf-of-module complexes]
1301	  \label{lem:quasiIso_of_evaluation}
1302	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.quasiIso_of_evaluation,
1303	    AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_Fmap_homologyMap,
1304	    AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_of_evaluation}
1305	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
1306	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1307	  % (proof, the sectionwise-reduction step) (read from
1308	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1220--1226).
1309	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "It suffices to show that taking sections of this extended
1310	  % complex over any open $W \subset X$ leads to an acyclic complex."
1311	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1312	  (sectionwise reduction).}
1313	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of rings on a category \(C\) and let
1314	  \(\varphi : K \to L\) be a morphism of chain complexes in the abelian category
1315	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) of presheaves of \(R\)-modules. Suppose that for \emph{every}
1316	  object \(V\) the evaluated morphism — the image of \(\varphi\) under the functor
1317	  \(\bigl(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\bigr).
1318	  \operatorname{mapHomologicalComplex}\), a morphism of complexes of
1319	  \(R(V)\)-modules — is a quasi-isomorphism. Then \(\varphi\) is a
1320	  quasi-isomorphism. This is the objectwise-reduction step that turns the
1321	  resolution claim of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso} into a family of
1322	  sectionwise (combinatorial) statements: homology in \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) is
1323	  computed open-by-open, because the evaluation functors
1324	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\) are exact (hence preserve
1325	  homology) and jointly conservative. The Lean entry point is
1326	  \(\operatorname{quasiIso\_of\_evaluation}\), which combines the joint
1327	  conservativity of the evaluations (each \(g\) is an isomorphism as soon as every
1328	  \((\operatorname{evaluation} R\, V).\operatorname{map} g\) is) with the fact that
1329	  each \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\) preserves homology.
1330	\end{lemma}
1331	
1332	\begin{proof}
1333	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
1334	  A morphism of complexes is a quasi-isomorphism iff it induces an isomorphism on
1335	  homology in every degree (\(\operatorname{quasiIso\_iff}\),
1336	  \(\operatorname{quasiIsoAt\_iff\_isIso\_homologyMap}\)). Fix a degree \(i\). The
1337	  homology map \(H_i(\varphi)\) is an isomorphism in \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) iff each of
1338	  its evaluations is an isomorphism, since the underlying
1339	  presheaf-of-abelian-groups functor reflects isomorphisms and a morphism of
1340	  presheaves is an isomorphism iff it is so objectwise (joint conservativity). For
1341	  each \(V\), evaluation preserves homology, so the evaluation of \(H_i(\varphi)\)
1342	  is the homology map of the evaluated complex morphism; the latter is an
1343	  isomorphism because the evaluated morphism is a quasi-isomorphism by hypothesis.
1344	  Hence \(H_i(\varphi)\) is an isomorphism for all \(i\), i.e.\ \(\varphi\) is a
1345	  quasi-isomorphism.
1346	\end{proof}

1348	\begin{lemma}
1349	
1350	\leanok
1351	[Sectionwise description of the evaluated free complex]
1352	  \label{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}
1353	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEval_X}
1354	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:cover_structure_presheaf}
1355	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1356	  % (proof, the sectionwise description of $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$) (read from
1357	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1227--1252).
1358	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Write $I = I_1 \amalg I_2$ where $W \subset U_i$ if and only
1359	  % if $i \in I_1$. ... If $I_1 \not = \emptyset$, then
1360	  % $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)$ and
1361	  % $K^{ext}_p(W) = \bigoplus\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p \in I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(W)$.
1362	  % This is true because of the simple description of the presheaves
1363	  % $(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}$."
1364	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1365	  (description of the evaluated complex).}
1366	  Fix an open \(V \subset X\) and write \(I_1 = I_1(V) = \{\,i \in I : V \leq U_i\,\}\)
1367	  for the set of cover members containing \(V\). Evaluating the free presheaf complex
1368	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex} at
1369	  \(V\) gives, degreewise,
1370	  \[
1371	    K(\mathcal{U})_p(V)
1372	      = \bigoplus_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I}
1373	        \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_\sigma)(V)
1374	      = \bigoplus_{\substack{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I \\ V \leq U_\sigma}}
1375	        \mathcal{O}_X(V)
1376	      = \bigoplus_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(V),
1377	  \]
1378	  because \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,W)(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) when
1379	  \(V \leq W\) and \(0\) otherwise (poset hom-set is a subsingleton), and
1380	  \(V \leq U_\sigma = U_{\sigma(0)} \cap \cdots \cap U_{\sigma(p)}\) holds iff
1381	  \(\sigma\) takes all its values in \(I_1\). Under this identification the
1382	  differential of \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet(V)\) is the alternating-sum combinatorial
1383	  {\v C}ech differential of the full simplex on the index set \(I_1\) with
1384	  coefficients in the single module \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\). Moreover the evaluated
1385	  augmentation target is \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) when
1386	  \(I_1 \neq \varnothing\) and \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = 0\) when
1387	  \(I_1 = \varnothing\). Thus the evaluation at \(V\) of the augmented complex
1388	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet \to \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}[0]\)
1389	  (\(\operatorname{cechFreeComplexAug}\)) is precisely the augmented combinatorial
1390	  {\v C}ech complex of the simplex on \(I_1\) with coefficients \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\).
1391	  The Lean entry point for the degree-\(p\) unfolding is
1392	  \(\operatorname{cechFreePresheafComplex\_X}\) (which exposes
1393	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_p = \coprod_\sigma \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_\sigma)\) by
1394	  \(\operatorname{rfl}\)) composed with
1395	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation}\) at \(V\).
1396	\end{lemma}
1397	
1398	\begin{proof}
1399	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:cover_structure_presheaf}
1400	  \leanok
1401	  The degreewise identity is the value at \(V\) of the explicit description of the
1402	  free presheaf \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,W)\) recorded in
1403	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}: it sends \(V\) to the free
1404	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-module on \(\operatorname{Hom}(V, W)\), which is
1405	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) if \(V \leq W\) and \(0\) otherwise. Applying
1406	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation}\;V\) to the coproduct decomposition
1407	  \(\operatorname{cechFreePresheafComplex\_X}\) commutes with the direct sum and
1408	  collapses each summand to \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) or \(0\) according to whether
1409	  \(V \leq U_\sigma\), i.e.\ whether \(\sigma\) factors through \(I_1\); the surviving
1410	  summands are exactly those indexed by \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1\). The
1411	  evaluated faces are the index-dropping restriction maps, which on the surviving
1412	  summands are identities of \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\), so the alternating sum is the
1413	  combinatorial {\v C}ech differential. The augmentation value follows from
1414	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) exactly when some \(U_i\)
1415	  contains \(V\) (\(I_1 \neq \varnothing\)), recorded in
1416	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_structure_presheaf}.
1417	\end{proof}

1422	[Evaluated free complex over an open meeting no cover member]
1423	  \label{lem:cech_free_eval_empty}

1425	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}
1426	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1427	  % (proof, the empty case) (read from references/stacks-cohomology.tex,
1428	  % L1228--1230).
1429	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "If $I_1 = \emptyset$, then the complex $K^{ext}_\bullet(W) = 0$
1430	  % so there is nothing to prove."
1431	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1432	  (empty case).}
1433	  With the notation of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}, suppose
1434	  \(I_1(V) = \varnothing\), i.e.\ \(V\) is contained in no cover member \(U_i\). Then
1435	  the evaluated augmented complex is identically zero: every degree-\(p\) term
1436	  \(\bigoplus_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) is the empty
1437	  direct sum \(0\) (there is no map \(\mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \varnothing\)) and the
1438	  augmentation target is \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = 0\). Consequently the
1439	  evaluation at \(V\) of \(\operatorname{cechFreeComplexAug}\) is a morphism of zero
1440	  complexes, which is a quasi-isomorphism (both sides have vanishing homology).
1441	\end{lemma}
1442	
1443	\begin{proof}
1444	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}
1445	  \leanok
1446	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise} each evaluated term is
1447	  \(\bigoplus_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(V)\); when
1448	  \(I_1 = \varnothing\) the index type \(\mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1\) is empty, so the
1449	  term is the zero object, and likewise \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = 0\). A
1450	  morphism whose source and target complexes are both zero (so all their homology
1451	  objects are zero, \(\operatorname{IsZero}\)) is a quasi-isomorphism; in Lean this is
1452	  immediate from \(\operatorname{quasiIso\_iff}\) since each
1453	  \(\operatorname{homologyMap}\) is a map of zero objects, hence an isomorphism.
1454	\end{proof}

1466	  % $h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}} =
1467	  % \left\{ \begin{matrix}
1468	  % 0 & \text{if} & i_0 \not = i_{\text{fix}} \\
1469	  % s_{i_1 \ldots i_{p + 1}} & \text{if} & i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}

1471	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1472	  (the contracting map \(h\)).}
1473	  With the notation of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}, suppose
1474	  \(I_1(V) \neq \varnothing\) and fix an index \(i_{\mathrm{fix}} \in I_1\). The
1475	  \emph{prepend homotopy} is the family of \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-linear maps
1476	  \[
1477	    h_p : K(\mathcal{U})_p(V) \longrightarrow K(\mathcal{U})_{p+1}(V),
1478	    \qquad
1479	    h_p(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} =
1480	      \begin{cases}
1481	        s_{i_1 \ldots i_{p+1}} & i_0 = i_{\mathrm{fix}}, \\
1482	        0 & i_0 \neq i_{\mathrm{fix}},
1483	      \end{cases}
1484	  \]
1485	  defined on the \(I_1\)-indexed direct sum of
1486	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise} as the \(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}\) of
1487	  the per-summand prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) maps. This is exactly the
1488	  combinatorial content of \(\operatorname{CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy}\)
1489	  (the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) map already used in the
1490	  affine vanishing Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}), here with the single
1491	  coefficient module \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\). The maps \(h_p\) (together with the
1492	  degree \((-1)\!\to\!0\) map onto \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\))
1493	  are the homotopy hom maps assembled into a
1494	  \(\operatorname{HomologicalComplex.Homotopy}\) in
1495	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty}.

1501	  evaluated term is a finite direct sum of copies of \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) indexed by
1502	  \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1\). The prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) prescription

1511	\begin{lemma}
1512	[The prepend homotopy contracts the augmented evaluated complex]
1513	  \label{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy_spec}
1514	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEvalPrependHomotopy_spec}
1515	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy}
1516	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1517	  % (proof, the $dh + hd = \mathrm{id}$ computation) (read from
1518	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1272--1285).
1519	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Then we compute
1520	  % $(dh + hd)(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_0 \ldots i_p} +
1521	  % \sum_{j \geq 1}\sum_{i \in I_1}
1522	  % (-1)^j (i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}) s_{i_1 \ldots i_{j - 1} i i_j \ldots i_p}
1523	  % + (i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}) d(s)_{i_1 \ldots i_p}$
1524	  % which is equal to $s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}$ as desired."
1525	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1526	  (the contracting identity).}
1527	  In the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy}
1528	  (\(I_1(V) \neq \varnothing\), \(i_{\mathrm{fix}} \in I_1\) fixed), the prepend
1529	  homotopy \(h\) satisfies the contracting identity
1530	  \[
1531	    d \circ h + h \circ d = \mathrm{id}
1532	  \]
1533	  on the augmented evaluated complex
1534	  \(\cdots \to K(\mathcal{U})_1(V) \to K(\mathcal{U})_0(V) \to
1535	  \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) \to 0\) (including the degree-\(0\)/degree-\((-1)\)
1536	  augmentation term). Here \(d\) is the alternating-sum {\v C}ech differential of
1537	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}.
1538	\end{lemma}
1539	
1540	\begin{proof}
1541	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy}
1542	  This is the same alternating-sum cancellation as
1543	  \(\operatorname{CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy\_spec}\)
1544	  (\(\operatorname{combDifferential}(\operatorname{combHomotopy} r\, t) +
1545	  \operatorname{combHomotopy} r\, (\operatorname{combDifferential} t) = t\)),
1546	  specialised to coefficients \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\). Expanding
1547	  \((dh + hd)(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\): the \(j = 0\) term of \(dh\) prepends and then
1548	  removes \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\), returning \(s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\); every remaining term
1549	  of \(dh\) cancels in pairs against the corresponding term of \(hd\) (the
1550	  prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) of the differential), because removing an index
1551	  \(j \geq 1\) after prepending \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) equals prepending
1552	  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) after removing index \(j-1\), with opposite signs. The net
1553	  result is \(s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\), i.e.\ \(dh + hd = \mathrm{id}\); the index
1554	  bookkeeping is the \(\operatorname{cons\_comp\_succAbove\_succ}\) /
1555	  \(\operatorname{combSign\_flip}\) content already discharged for the affine port.
1556	\end{proof}

1684	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V)\). Split into two cases on \(I_1\). If
1685	  \(I_1 = \varnothing\), Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_empty} shows both sides vanish
1686	  and the evaluated augmentation is a quasi-isomorphism. If \(I_1 \neq \varnothing\),
1687	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty} provides the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)
1688	  contracting homotopy, exhibiting the augmented evaluated complex as contractible and
1689	  hence the evaluated augmentation as a quasi-isomorphism. In either case the
1690	  hypothesis of Lemma~\ref{lem:quasiIso_of_evaluation} holds for \(V\), so
1691	  \(\operatorname{cechFreeComplexAug}\) is a quasi-isomorphism; equivalently the
1692	  homology presheaves of \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) vanish away from degree \(0\) and
1693	  equal \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) in degree \(0\).
1694	
1695	  The contracting homotopy is built directly at the \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-module
1696	  (section) level as the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) map — the same combinatorial
1697	  content as \(\operatorname{CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy}\) /
1698	  \(\operatorname{combHomotopy\_spec}\) used for the
1699	  affine vanishing Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}.
1700	\end{proof}
1701	
1702	\begin{lemma}[Right adjoint of a mono-preserving left adjoint preserves injectives]
1703	  \label{lem:injective_of_adjoint}
1704	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint}
1705	  \mathlibok
1706	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
1707	  (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Preadditive.Injective.Basic}).}
1708	  Let \(L \dashv R\) be an adjunction between abelian (or, more generally, suitable)
1709	  categories such that the left adjoint \(L\) preserves monomorphisms. Then for every
1710	  injective object \(J\) of the target, \(R(J)\) is an injective object of the source.
1711	\end{lemma}
1712	
1713	\begin{lemma}[Sheafification is left adjoint to the inclusion of sheaves of modules]
1714	  \label{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}
1715	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction}
1716	  \mathlibok
1717	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
1718	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification}).}
1719	  For the identity ring map \(\alpha = \mathbf{1}\) over a scheme \(X\),
1720	  sheafification is left adjoint to the inclusion
1721	  \(\mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \hookrightarrow \mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) of
1722	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules into presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules:
1723	  \[
1724	    \operatorname{sheafification} \;\dashv\; \iota,
1725	    \qquad
1726	    \iota = \text{the inclusion } X.\mathrm{Modules} \hookrightarrow
1727	    X.\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}.
1728	  \]
1729	  The left adjoint sheafification is exact, in particular it preserves
1730	  monomorphisms.
1731	\end{lemma}
1732	
1733	\begin{lemma}
1734	[Injective modules are {\v C}ech-acyclic]
1735	  \label{lem:injective_cech_acyclic}
1736	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.injective_cech_acyclic,
1737	    AlgebraicGeometry.injective_toPresheafOfModules}
1738	  \uses{def:cech_complex, def:section_cech_complex, lem:cech_complex_hom_identification, lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso, lem:injective_of_adjoint, lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}
1739	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma
1740	  % \texttt{lemma-injective-trivial-cech} (statement + proof) (read from
1741	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1407--1431).
1742	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a ringed space.
1743	  % Let $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i$ be an open covering.
1744	  % Let $\mathcal{I}$ be an injective $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
1745	  % Then
1746	  % $$
1747	  % \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) =
1748	  % \left\{
1749	  % \begin{matrix}
1750	  % \mathcal{I}(U) & \text{if} & p = 0 \\
1751	  % 0 & \text{if} & p > 0
1752	  % \end{matrix}
1753	  % \right.
1754	  % $$"
1755	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology,
1756	  \texttt{lemma-injective-trivial-cech}.}
1757	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space, let \(\mathcal{U} : U =
1758	  \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be any open covering, and let \(\mathcal{I}\) be an
1759	  \emph{injective} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then the {\v C}ech cohomology of
1760	  \(\mathcal{I}\) is concentrated in degree zero:
1761	  \[
1762	    \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) =
1763	    \begin{cases}
1764	      \mathcal{I}(U) & p = 0, \\
1765	      0 & p > 0.
1766	    \end{cases}
1767	  \]
1768	\end{lemma}
1769	
1770	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "An injective $\mathcal{O}_X$-module is also injective as an
1771	% object in
1772	% the category $\textit{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)$ (for example since
1773	% sheafification is an exact left adjoint to the inclusion functor,
1774	% using Homology, Lemma \ref{homology-lemma-adjoint-preserve-injectives}).
1775	% Hence we can apply Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-derived-presheaves}
1776	% (or its proof) to see the result."
1777	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Let $\mathcal{I}$ be an injective presheaf of
1778	% $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules.
1779	% In this case the functor $\Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(-, \mathcal{I})$
1780	% is exact on $\textit{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)$. By
1781	% Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-map-into} we have
1782	% $$
1783	% \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet, \mathcal{I})
1784	% =
1785	% \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}).
1786	% $$
1787	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-homology-complex} we have that $K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet$ is
1788	% quasi-isomorphic to $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}[0]$. Hence by
1789	% the exactness of Hom into $\mathcal{I}$ mentioned above we see
1790	% that $\check{H}^i(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0$ for all
1791	% $i > 0$."
1792	\begin{proof}
1793	  \uses{def:cech_complex, def:section_cech_complex, lem:cech_complex_hom_identification, lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso, lem:injective_of_adjoint, lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}
1794	  The argument has two independent parts.
1795	
1796	  \emph{Part 1: an injective sheaf is injective as a presheaf.} Sheafification is
1797	  left adjoint to the inclusion
1798	  \(\iota : \mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \hookrightarrow \mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)
1799	  of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules into presheaves of
1800	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules (Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}), and its left
1801	  adjoint sheafification is exact, hence preserves monomorphisms. By
1802	  Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint} the right adjoint \(\iota\) carries injective
1803	  objects to injective objects; thus an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module
1804	  \(\mathcal{I}\) is injective as an object of \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\). In
1805	  particular the contravariant functor \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(-,
1806	  \mathcal{I})\) is exact on \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\).
1807	
1808	  \emph{Part 2: vanishing.} By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso} the free
1809	  presheaf complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of
1810	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex} is a resolution of
1811	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) concentrated in degree \(0\); equivalently the
1812	  augmented complex \(\cdots \to K(\mathcal{U})_1 \to K(\mathcal{U})_0 \to
1813	  \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}} \to 0\) is exact in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\).
1814	  Applying the exact functor \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(-, \mathcal{I})\)
1815	  of Part 1 to this exact augmented complex yields an exact complex; by the
1816	  identification \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet,
1817	  \mathcal{I}) = \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I})\) of
1818	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_hom_identification} (with the section {\v C}ech complex
1819	  of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex}), this is precisely the statement that
1820	  the section {\v C}ech complex of \(\mathcal{I}\) is exact in positive degrees, with
1821	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}, \mathcal{I}) =
1822	  \mathcal{I}(U)\) supplying degree \(0\). Hence
1823	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\) and
1824	  \(\check{H}^0(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = \mathcal{I}(U)\). This direct route uses
1825	  neither enough-injectives in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) nor a right-derived
1826	  \(\delta\)-functor identification.
1827	\end{proof}
1828	
1829	\begin{lemma}
1830	[Surjectivity on sections from cofinal {\v C}ech-$H^1$ vanishing]
1831	  \label{lem:ses_cech_h1}
1832	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.ses_cech_h1}
1833	  \uses{def:cech_complex}

1227	$K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$ is homotopy equivalent to the zero complex.
1228	Write $I = I_1 \amalg I_2$ where $W \subset U_i$ if and only
1229	if $i \in I_1$.
1230	
1231	\medskip\noindent
1232	If $I_1 = \emptyset$, then the complex $K^{ext}_\bullet(W) = 0$ so there is
1233	nothing to prove.
1234	
1235	\medskip\noindent
1236	If $I_1 \not = \emptyset$, then
1237	$\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)$
1238	and
1239	$$
1240	K^{ext}_p(W) =
1241	\bigoplus\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p \in I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(W).
1242	$$
1243	This is true because of the simple description of the presheaves
1244	$(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}$.
1245	Moreover, the differential of the complex $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$
1246	is given by
1247	$$
1248	d(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} =
1249	\sum\nolimits_{j = 0, \ldots, p + 1} \sum\nolimits_{i \in I_1}
1250	(-1)^j s_{i_0 \ldots i_{j - 1} i i_j \ldots i_p}.
1251	$$
1252	The sum is finite as the element $s$ has finite support.
1253	Fix an element $i_{\text{fix}} \in I_1$. Define a map
1254	$$
1255	h : K^{ext}_p(W) \longrightarrow K^{ext}_{p + 1}(W)
1256	$$
1257	by the rule
1258	$$
1259	h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}} =
1260	\left\{
1261	\begin{matrix}
1262	0 & \text{if} & i_0 \not = i_{\text{fix}} \\
1263	s_{i_1 \ldots i_{p + 1}} & \text{if} & i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}
1264	\end{matrix}
1265	\right.
1266	$$
1267	We will use the shorthand
1268	$h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}} = (i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}) s_{i_1 \ldots i_p}$
1269	for this. Then we compute
1270	\begin{eqnarray*}
1271	& & (dh + hd)(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \\
1272	& = &
1273	\sum_j \sum_{i \in I_1} (-1)^j h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{j - 1} i i_j \ldots i_p}
1274	+
1275	(i = i_0) d(s)_{i_1 \ldots i_p} \\
1276	& = &
1277	s_{i_0 \ldots i_p} +
1278	\sum_{j \geq 1}\sum_{i \in I_1}
1279	(-1)^j (i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}) s_{i_1 \ldots i_{j - 1} i i_j \ldots i_p}
1280	+
1281	(i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}) d(s)_{i_1 \ldots i_p}
1282	\end{eqnarray*}
1283	which is equal to $s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}$ as desired.
1284	\end{proof}

1286	\begin{lemma}

295	cosimplicial map `f : [m] ⟶ [n]` acts at multi-index `σ : Fin (n+1) → ι` by projecting to
296	the coordinate `σ ∘ f` and then restricting along `⨅ₖ U (σ k) ≤ ⨅ₗ U (σ (f l))`. This is the
297	covariant ({\v C}ech) variance that the alternating-coface-map complex turns into a cochain
298	complex.
299	
300	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules. Built
301	as an honest `SimplexCategory ⥤ Ab` functor so that `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` supplies
302	`d² = 0` for free (no hand-rolled alternating-sum identity). -/
303	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
304	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
305	  obj n := ∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
306	  map {m n} f := Pi.lift (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
307	    Pi.π _ (σ ∘ f.toOrderHom) ≫
308	      F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l)))).op)
309	  map_id n := by
310	    apply Pi.hom_ext
311	    intro σ
312	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, SimplexCategory.id_toOrderHom,
313	      OrderHom.id_coe, Function.comp_id]
314	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (𝟙 _)]
315	    simp
316	  map_comp {l m n} f g := by
317	    apply Pi.hom_ext
318	    intro σ
319	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom, OrderHom.comp_coe]
320	    rw [← Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
321	    congr 1
322	
323	/-- **Section {\v C}ech cochain complex of a presheaf of modules**
324	(blueprint `def:section_cech_complex`).
325	
326	The cochain complex `Č•(𝒰, F)` of abelian groups with degree-`p` term
327	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` and the alternating-sum-of-restrictions differential,
328	obtained as the alternating-coface-map complex of `sectionCechCosimplicial`. This is the
329	section complex of the blueprint, kept distinct from the relative pushforward `CechComplex`;
330	it is the right-hand side of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
331	`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`.
332	
333	Project-local: no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules exists in Mathlib. -/
334	noncomputable def sectionCechComplex {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
335	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
336	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)
337	
338	end AlgebraicGeometry

=== namespace context around 380-420 of bridge ===
homology-preserving functor. -/

section InjectiveHomExact

open CategoryTheory Limits

variable {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C]

/-- **`Hom(-, I)` preserves finite colimits for injective `I`.** For an injective object `I`
of an abelian category, the covariant functor `preadditiveYoneda.obj I : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab`
preserves finite colimits: it is left exact (preserves finite limits) automatically, and
injectivity makes it preserve epimorphisms, which together with left exactness gives
exactness. Combined with the automatic `PreservesFiniteLimits` instance this yields
`(preadditiveYoneda.obj I).PreservesHomology` by `Functor.preservesHomologyOfExact`.

Project-local: the exactness of `Hom(-, I)` for injective `I` is the categorical engine of
`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`. -/
instance preadditiveYoneda_obj_preservesFiniteColimits_of_injective
    (I : C) [Injective I] : PreservesFiniteColimits (preadditiveYoneda.obj I) := by
  have hepi : (preadditiveYoneda.obj I).PreservesEpimorphisms :=
    (Injective.injective_iff_preservesEpimorphisms_preadditiveYoneda_obj I).mp inferInstance
  rw [Functor.preservesFiniteColimits_iff_forall_exact_map_and_epi]
  intro S hS
  have hg := hS.epi_g
  exact ⟨hS.exact.map_of_mono_of_preservesKernel _ hS.mono_f inferInstance,
    hepi.preserves S.g⟩

/-- **`Hom(-, I)` carries quasi-isomorphisms to quasi-isomorphisms for injective `I`.**
Since `preadditiveYoneda.obj I` preserves homology
(`preadditiveYoneda_obj_preservesFiniteColimits_of_injective`), applying it degreewise to a
quasi-isomorphism `φ` of chain complexes in `Cᵒᵖ` produces a quasi-isomorphism of the
`Hom`-cochain complexes. This is the form consumed by `injective_cech_acyclic`: the
augmentation `cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰` (a quasi-isomorphism once Lane 1 lands), taken opposite
and mapped through `preadditiveYoneda.obj F`, stays a quasi-isomorphism.

Project-local: packages `quasiIsoAt_map_of_preservesHomology` against the injective-hom
exactness instance above. -/
lemma quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective (I : C) [Injective I]
    {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} {K L : HomologicalComplex Cᵒᵖ c}
    (φ : K ⟶ L) [QuasiIso φ] :
    QuasiIso (((preadditiveYoneda.obj I).mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ) := by

AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechAcyclic.lean:1150:        iInf_fin_succ]

1125	
1126	/-- Splitting an indexed infimum over `Fin (n+1)` into the `0`-th term and the
1127	infimum over the tail.  Lattice-combinatorial helper behind `basicOpen_sprod`. -/
1128	private lemma iInf_fin_succ {α : Type*} [CompleteLattice α] (n : ℕ) (f : Fin (n + 1) → α) :
1129	    (⨅ i, f i) = f 0 ⊓ ⨅ i : Fin n, f i.succ := by
1130	  apply le_antisymm
1131	  · exact le_inf (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iInf fun i => iInf_le _ i.succ)
1132	  · refine le_iInf fun i => ?_
1133	    refine Fin.cases inf_le_left (fun j => le_trans inf_le_right (iInf_le _ j)) i
1134	
1135	/-- **Multi-index basic-open intersection** (geometric input to step (c)): the
1136	`(p+1)`-fold intersection of the basic opens `D(s_{σ k})` of a {\v C}ech multi-index
1137	`σ : Fin n → ι` is the basic open `D(s_σ)` of the product `s_σ = ∏ₖ s_{σ k}`.  This
1138	identifies the section group `F(⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k}))` of `sectionCechCosimplicial` with the
1139	away localisation `M_{s_σ}` of `SectionCechModule.dCoeff`. -/
1140	lemma basicOpen_sprod {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u} (n : ℕ) (s : ι → R) (σ : Fin n → ι) :
1141	    (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)) : (Spec R).Opens)
1142	      = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (∏ k, s (σ k)) := by
1143	  induction n with
1144	  | zero =>
1145	      rw [show (∏ k, s (σ k)) = 1 from Finset.prod_of_isEmpty _]
1146	      simp only [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one]
1147	      exact iInf_of_empty _
1148	  | succ m ih =>
1149	      rw [Fin.prod_univ_succ, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul, ← ih (fun i => σ i.succ),
1150	        iInf_fin_succ]
1151	      rfl
1152	
1153	/-- **Quasi-coherent sections over a {\v C}ech intersection are an away localisation**
1154	(`def:qcoh_sections_localized`, tilde case — step (b) of the L1 bridge).  For the
1155	standard sheaf `tilde M` of an `R`-module `M`, the section-restriction map from `M`
1156	to the sections over the `(p+1)`-fold basic-open intersection
1157	`⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k}) = D(s_σ)` exhibits that section group as the away localisation
1158	`M_{s_σ}` (`IsLocalizedModule` for `Submonoid.powers (∏ₖ s_{σ k})`).  Combines the
1159	Mathlib instance `IsLocalizedModule (.powers g) (tilde.toOpen M (D g)).hom` with the
1160	intersection identification `basicOpen_sprod`.  This is the degreewise section
1161	identification that `lem:section_cech_homology_exact` consumes; the restriction
1162	compatibility (item (5) of the blueprint definition) is `tilde.toOpen_res` together
1163	with `qcohRestriction_eq_comparison` below. -/
1164	lemma qcohSectionsAwayLocalized {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u}
1165	    (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {n : ℕ} (s : ι → R) (σ : Fin n → ι) :

1167	      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom := by

634	  % the complex of localisations) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
635	  % L58--67).
636	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
637	  % $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
638	  % is identified with the complex
639	  % $$
640	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
641	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to

645	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
646	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (identification with the
647	  complex of localisations).}
648	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec}
649	  R}\)-module and \(\mathcal{U}\) the standard cover associated with a spanning family
650	  \(s : \iota \to R\). Under the section identification of
651	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}, the section {\v C}ech complex
652	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
653	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} is isomorphic, as a cochain complex, to
654	  the concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules
655	  \[
656	    D^\bullet : \quad
657	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
658	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0,1\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
659	    \cdots,
660	    \qquad
661	    s_\sigma = \prod_{k} s_{\sigma(k)},
662	  \]
663	  whose differential is the alternating sum of the canonical localisation maps. For
664	  each \(p\), vanishing of the abstract homology is equivalent to exactness of the
665	  underlying \(R\)-module maps,
666	  \[
667	    \operatorname{IsZero}\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
668	      \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p\bigr)
669	    \;\Longleftrightarrow\;
670	    \operatorname{Function.Exact}\bigl(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to D^{p+1}\bigr).
671	  \]
672	  The two directions are supplied by Mathlib: \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the
673	  homology is \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) at \(p\)
674	  (\(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\)), and \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\)
675	  of a short complex of \(R\)-modules is \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) of the
676	  underlying linear maps (\(\operatorname{ShortComplex.moduleCat\_exact\_iff}\)).
677	\end{lemma}
678	
679	\begin{proof}
680	  \uses{def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_complex}
681	  The term identification is the degreewise statement of
682	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: the degree-\(p\) section group
683	  \(\prod_\sigma \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is \(\prod_\sigma M_{s_\sigma}\), since
684	  each \((p+1)\)-fold intersection \(D(s_{\sigma(0)}) \cap \cdots \cap
685	  D(s_{\sigma(p)})\) is the basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product
686	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\) and the sections there are the away
687	  localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\). The differential identification is item~(5) of
688	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: each face restriction
689	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j})) \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is the
690	  canonical localisation map \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\), so the
691	  alternating sum of restrictions is the alternating sum of localisation maps, the
692	  differential of \(D^\bullet\). This gives the isomorphism of cochain complexes
693	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \cong D^\bullet\). The
694	  homology equivalence then follows by transporting along this isomorphism and
695	  applying \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\) (\(\operatorname{IsZero}\)
696	  homology \(\Leftrightarrow\) \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\)) and
697	  \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.moduleCat\_exact\_iff}\) (\(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) of
698	  \(R\)-modules \(\Leftrightarrow\) \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\)).
699	
700	  The identification of the abstract section complex with the concrete localised
701	  complex \(D^\bullet\) --- together with the construction of the prepend transport
702	  \(c\) and the localisation cofaces \(\delta_k\) discharging the unit, shift, and
703	  coface-commutation compatibilities \(\mathtt{hu}\)/\(\mathtt{hsh}\)/\(\mathtt{hcomm}\)
704	  of the dependent combinatorial port --- is the bulk of the bridge. The non-circular
705	  route is reflection along the localisation equivalence on its essential image (the
706	  global-sections functor \(\Gamma\) is the inverse equivalence there, so it reflects
707	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of homology); it is \emph{not} the assertion that
708	  ``\(\Gamma\) preserves homology'', which would presuppose the very affine exactness
709	  being proved.
710	\end{proof}

whose differential is the alternating sum of the canonical localisation maps. The
  intended top-level conclusion is the directed homology vanishing
  \[
    1 \leq p
    \;\Longrightarrow\;
    \operatorname{IsZero}\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
      \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p\bigr),
  \]
  which is the form the affine vanishing
  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} (\(\operatorname{sectionCech\_affine\_vanishing}\))
  consumes; it is read off from positive-degree exactness of the underlying group
  homomorphisms,
  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\bigl(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to D^{p+1}\bigr)\), which is
  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}.

  The reduction from the abstract homology to this group-level exactness is \emph{not}
  a \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}\) statement: the degree-\(p\) object of
  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) is the \emph{categorical
  product}
  \(\prod^{c}_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \iota}
  \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) in the category \(\mathrm{Ab}\) of abelian groups (the
  alternating-coface-map complex of the \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-valued cosimplicial object
  \(\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\)), not an \(R\)-module object, so the
  module-category criterion \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.moduleCat\_exact\_iff}\) does
  not apply. The correct chain runs through three steps, isolated as the sub-lemmas
  below: \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the homology is \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) at
  \(p\) (\(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\)); \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\)
  of a short complex in \(\mathrm{Ab}\) is the underlying-group exactness
  \(\forall x_2,\; g(x_2) = 0 \Rightarrow \exists x_1,\; f(x_1) = x_2\) via the abelian
  analogue \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}); and the categorical product is moved to the
  per-\(\sigma\) localised modules by the element-level product equivalence
  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\prod^{c} F_\sigma) \simeq \prod_\sigma
  \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma)\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}), under which the {\v C}ech coface
  differential matches the localisation differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of
  \(D^\bullet\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}). Transporting the
  already-proved exactness \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\) of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact} across the product equivalence then
  discharges the group-level exactness.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}
[Element-level product equivalence for the section {\v C}ech terms]
  \label{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechProductEquiv}
  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
  Let \(\{F_\sigma\}_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \iota}\) be the finite family of
  abelian groups \(F_\sigma = \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) appearing in degree \(p\) of
  the section {\v C}ech complex of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex}. There is
  an equivalence of underlying types
  \[
    \operatorname{ToType}\Bigl(\prod^{c}_{\sigma} F_\sigma\Bigr)
    \;\simeq\;
    \prod_{\sigma} \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma),
  \]
  between the elements of the categorical product \(\prod^{c}_{\sigma} F_\sigma\) in
  \(\mathrm{Ab}\) and the dependent functions \(\sigma \mapsto\) (element of
  \(F_\sigma\)). It is supplied by the Mathlib concrete-limit comparison
  \(\operatorname{CategoryTheory.Limits.Concrete.productEquiv}\), which holds because the
  forgetful functor \(\operatorname{forget}\,\mathrm{Ab}\) preserves the discrete
  product (\(\operatorname{PreservesLimit}(\operatorname{Discrete.functor} F)\)). Under
  this equivalence each coordinate
  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma)))\) is identified with the away
  localisation \(M_{s_\sigma} = \operatorname{dCoeff}\) of
  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex} by the
  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) uniqueness of
  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} (both
  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) and \(M_{s_\sigma}\) localise \(M\) at \(s_\sigma\), so
  the comparison is the canonical isomorphism). This is the move between the
  categorical product of \(\mathrm{Ab}\) and the per-\(\sigma\) localised modules.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
  The type-level equivalence is \(\operatorname{Concrete.productEquiv}\) applied to the
  family \(\sigma \mapsto F_\sigma\); its hypothesis is that
  \(\operatorname{forget}\,\mathrm{Ab}\) preserves the discrete-diagram limit, which is
  the standard instance for \(\mathrm{Ab}\). The per-coordinate identification
  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))) \cong M_{s_\sigma}\) is the
  uniqueness clause of \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\): by
  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} the restriction map
  \(M \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) and the localisation unit
  \(M \to M_{s_\sigma}\) are both localisations of \(M\) at
  \(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers}(s_\sigma)\)
  (\(\operatorname{qcohSectionsAwayLocalized}\)), hence canonically isomorphic over
  \(M\). Assembling the coordinatewise isomorphisms after the product equivalence gives
  the stated identification.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[Coface match: the section {\v C}ech differential is the localisation differential]
  \label{lem:section_cech_coface_match}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechCofaceMatch}
  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_module_complex}
  In the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}, the degree-\(p\)
  differential of the section {\v C}ech complex
  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) --- the degree-\(p\) map of
  the alternating-coface-map complex
  \(\operatorname{AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex}\) of the cosimplicial
  object \(\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\) --- unfolds to the alternating sum
  \[
    d^p \;=\; \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\, \delta_j,
    \qquad
    \delta_j = \operatorname{Pi.lift}\bigl(\sigma \mapsto
      \operatorname{Pi.\pi}_{\sigma \circ d_j} \;\circ\;
      \mathcal{F}.\mathrm{map}\,(\text{restriction})^{\mathrm{op}}\bigr),
  \]
  whose \(j\)-th coface \(\delta_j\) projects to the coordinate \(\sigma \circ d_j\) and
  applies the presheaf restriction
  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j})) \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\). Under the
  product equivalence of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv} and the
  multi-index intersection identity \(\operatorname{basicOpen\_sprod}\), each such
  restriction is the away-localisation comparison
  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison}\) of
  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}
  (\(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\)), i.e.\ the coface
  \(\operatorname{dCoface}\) of \(D^\bullet\). Hence \(d^p\) is exactly the localisation
  differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of
  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex}, transported across the product
  equivalence.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:section_cech_module_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
  Unfold the degree-\(p\) map of
  \(\operatorname{alternatingCofaceMapComplex}\) on
  \(\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\): by construction it is the alternating sum
  \(\sum_j (-1)^j\) of the cosimplicial cofaces, and the cosimplicial map at coface
  \(d_j\) is the \(\operatorname{Pi.lift}\) whose \(\sigma\)-component projects to
  \(\sigma \circ d_j\) and restricts along
  \(\bigsqcap_k D(s_{\sigma(k)}) \leq \bigsqcap_l D(s_{(\sigma \circ d_j)(l)})\). The
  intersection identity \(\operatorname{basicOpen\_sprod}\) rewrites both products of
  basic opens as single basic opens \(D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j}) \supseteq D(s_\sigma)\),
  and \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\) identifies the presheaf
  restriction between the corresponding section groups with the away comparison
  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison}\); this is precisely
  \(\operatorname{dCoface}\). Summing with signs and transporting along the product
  equivalence of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv} matches \(d^p\) with
  \(\operatorname{dDiff}\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[Abelian-group exactness criterion for the section {\v C}ech complex]
  \label{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechAbExact}
  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, lem:section_cech_product_equiv, lem:section_cech_coface_match, lem:section_cech_module_exact}
  For \(1 \leq p\) the homology of the section {\v C}ech complex
  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} vanishes,
  \(\operatorname{IsZero}(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
  \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p)\). The reduction uses
  \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\) to pass to
  \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\), then the abelian analogue
  \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\) --- which states that a short complex
  \(S\) of abelian groups is exact iff for every \(x_2\) with \(g(x_2) = 0\) there is
  \(x_1\) with \(f(x_1) = x_2\) --- to pass to underlying-group exactness. The latter is
  the already-proved \(R\)-module exactness \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}) transported across the product
  equivalence of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv} with the differential match
  of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:section_cech_product_equiv, lem:section_cech_coface_match, lem:section_cech_module_exact}
  Fix \(p \geq 1\). By \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\) it suffices to
  prove \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) of the section complex at \(p\), i.e.\ exactness of
  the short complex \(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to D^{p+1}\) of abelian groups around degree
  \(p\). Since each term is a categorical product in \(\mathrm{Ab}\), exactness is
  governed by \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\): it is equivalent to
  \(\forall x_2,\; g(x_2) = 0 \Rightarrow \exists x_1,\; f(x_1) = x_2\) on the
  underlying group elements. Transport this surjectivity-onto-kernel statement across
  the element-level product equivalence of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
  (which identifies the product groups with \(\prod_\sigma M_{s_\sigma}\)) and the
  coface match of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match} (which identifies the
  differentials with \(\operatorname{dDiff}\)); the resulting statement is exactly
  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(\operatorname{dDiff})\) in positive degree, which is
  \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}.
\end{proof}

  \(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers}(s_\sigma)\)
  (\(\operatorname{qcohSectionsAwayLocalized}\)), hence canonically isomorphic over
  \(M\). Assembling the coordinatewise isomorphisms after the product equivalence gives
  the stated identification.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[Coface match: the section {\v C}ech differential is the localisation differential]
  \label{lem:section_cech_coface_match}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechCofaceMatch}
  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_module_complex}
  In the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}, the degree-\(p\)
  differential of the section {\v C}ech complex
  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) --- the degree-\(p\) map of
  the alternating-coface-map complex
  \(\operatorname{AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex}\) of the cosimplicial
  object \(\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\) --- unfolds to the alternating sum
  \[
    d^p \;=\; \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\, \delta_j,
    \qquad
    \delta_j = \operatorname{Pi.lift}\bigl(\sigma \mapsto
      \operatorname{Pi.\pi}_{\sigma \circ d_j} \;\circ\;
      \mathcal{F}.\mathrm{map}\,(\text{restriction})^{\mathrm{op}}\bigr),
  \]
  whose \(j\)-th coface \(\delta_j\) projects to the coordinate \(\sigma \circ d_j\) and
  applies the presheaf restriction
  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j})) \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\). Under the
  product equivalence of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv} and the
  multi-index intersection identity \(\operatorname{basicOpen\_sprod}\), each such
  restriction is the away-localisation comparison
  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison}\) of
  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}
  (\(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\)), i.e.\ the coface
  \(\operatorname{dCoface}\) of \(D^\bullet\). Hence \(d^p\) is exactly the localisation
  differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of
  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex}, transported across the product
  equivalence.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:section_cech_module_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
  Unfold the degree-\(p\) map of
  \(\operatorname{alternatingCofaceMapComplex}\) on
  \(\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\): by construction it is the alternating sum
  \(\sum_j (-1)^j\) of the cosimplicial cofaces, and the cosimplicial map at coface
  \(d_j\) is the \(\operatorname{Pi.lift}\) whose \(\sigma\)-component projects to
  \(\sigma \circ d_j\) and restricts along
  \(\bigsqcap_k D(s_{\sigma(k)}) \leq \bigsqcap_l D(s_{(\sigma \circ d_j)(l)})\). The
  intersection identity \(\operatorname{basicOpen\_sprod}\) rewrites both products of
  basic opens as single basic opens \(D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j}) \supseteq D(s_\sigma)\),
  and \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\) identifies the presheaf
  restriction between the corresponding section groups with the away comparison
  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison}\); this is precisely
  \(\operatorname{dCoface}\). Summing with signs and transporting along the product
  equivalence of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv} matches \(d^p\) with
  \(\operatorname{dDiff}\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[Abelian-group exactness criterion for the section {\v C}ech complex]
  \label{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechAbExact}
  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, lem:section_cech_product_equiv, lem:section_cech_coface_match, lem:section_cech_module_exact}
  For \(1 \leq p\) the homology of the section {\v C}ech complex
  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} vanishes,
  \(\operatorname{IsZero}(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
  \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p)\). The reduction uses
  \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\) to pass to
  \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\), then the abelian analogue
  \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\) --- which states that a short complex
  \(S\) of abelian groups is exact iff for every \(x_2\) with \(g(x_2) = 0\) there is
  \(x_1\) with \(f(x_1) = x_2\) --- to pass to underlying-group exactness. The latter is
  the already-proved \(R\)-module exactness \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}) transported across the product
  equivalence of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv} with the differential match
  of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:section_cech_product_equiv, lem:section_cech_coface_match, lem:section_cech_module_exact}
  Fix \(p \geq 1\). By \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\) it suffices to
  prove \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) of the section complex at \(p\), i.e.\ exactness of
  the short complex \(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to D^{p+1}\) of abelian groups around degree
  \(p\). Since each term is a categorical product in \(\mathrm{Ab}\), exactness is
  governed by \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\): it is equivalent to
  \(\forall x_2,\; g(x_2) = 0 \Rightarrow \exists x_1,\; f(x_1) = x_2\) on the
  underlying group elements. Transport this surjectivity-onto-kernel statement across
  the element-level product equivalence of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
  (which identifies the product groups with \(\prod_\sigma M_{s_\sigma}\)) and the
  coface match of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match} (which identifies the
  differentials with \(\operatorname{dDiff}\)); the resulting statement is exactly
  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(\operatorname{dDiff})\) in positive degree, which is
  \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}.
\end{proof}
